mission.title: Install and Configure Rancher / RKE2
mission.description: Rancher is a complete container management platform that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters. It is ideal for organizations looking to manage multiple Kubernetes clusters across different environments. Key trade-offs include the need for sufficient hardware resources and the complexity of managing multiple clusters.

steps.title: Install the Rancher CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Rancher CLI, which is necessary for managing Rancher from the command line.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/rancher/cli/releases/download/v2.13.3/rancher-darwin-amd64-v2.13.3.tar.gz

tar -xzf rancher-darwin-amd64-v2.13.3.tar.gz

sudo mv rancher /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the Rancher CLI for macOS.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using RKE2 with the specified version and node count.

```bash

curl -sfL https://get.rke2.io | sh -

sudo systemctl enable rke2-server

sudo systemctl start rke2-server

```

> Install RKE2 on the master node.
> Enable the RKE2 server to start on boot.
> Start the RKE2 server.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures kubeconfig to access the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

sudo cp /etc/rancher/rke2/rke2.yaml ~/.kube/config

sudo chown $(id -u):$(id -g) ~/.kube/config

```

> Copy the RKE2 kubeconfig to your home directory.
> Change ownership of the kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This step verifies that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster to ensure they are ready.

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up monitoring and autoscaling for the cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rancher/rancher/v2.13.3/manifests/rancher-monitoring.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rancher/rancher/v2.13.3/manifests/rancher-autoscaler.yaml

```

> Install Rancher monitoring.
> Install Rancher autoscaler.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Rancher
uninstall.description:
This step removes Rancher and all associated resources. Be aware that this will lead to data loss.

```bash

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rancher/rancher/v2.13.3/manifests/rancher.yaml

sudo systemctl stop rke2-server

```

> Delete the Rancher deployment.
> Stop the RKE2 server.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Rancher
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and verify the current state of the cluster.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces > backup-$(date +%F).yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rancher/rancher/v2.13.3/manifests/rancher.yaml

kubectl get pods -n cattle-system

```

> Backup current resources.
> Apply the new Rancher manifest.
> Verify that all Rancher pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:6443: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The RKE2 server is not running or has failed to start.

**Fix:**
Check the RKE2 server logs with 'sudo journalctl -u rke2-server -f' and restart the service if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found in default namespace'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster may not have any resources deployed.

**Fix:**
Deploy a sample application to verify the cluster is functioning.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** kubectl is not installed or not in the PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl using 'curl -LO https://dl.k8s.io/release/$(curl -L -s https://dl.k8s.io/release/stable.txt)/bin/linux/amd64/kubectl && chmod +x ./kubectl && sudo mv ./kubectl /usr/local/bin/'

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the API server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig file may not be set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the kubeconfig file is correctly copied and owned by the user with 'ls -l ~/.kube/config'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes v1.30, improved RBAC configurations, and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy API versions for certain resources.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v2.12.x, ensure to migrate any custom resource definitions to the new API versions.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
